text: Through physical and mechanical property tests and formula calculations on unprocessed soils at different depths, the basic physical and mechanical parameters of soil at different depths, such as natural density, moisture content, bulk density, porosity ratio, specific gravity, liquid plasticity limit, liquid plasticity index, permeability coefficient, compression coefficient and lateral pressure coefficient, friction Angle and cohesion, were obtained. The properties and indicators of the soil samples are listed in Table 2-1. It can be seen from Table 2-1 that the indicators of soil layers at different depths are different. Specifically, the soft soil in the estuary area is mainly composed of silt, silty soil and silt clay, with a relatively high moisture content, which can reach up to 69.1% at the highest and 24.9% at the lowest. The range of natural bulk density variation is 1.54 to 2.05. The plasticity index is all greater than 16.1, with the highest reaching 37.2. The liquid index is all greater than 0.43, with the highest reaching 1.37, indicating that the soft soil at the Pearl River estuary is relatively soft. It can be determined that the soft soil at the Pearl River estuary is in a fluid, soft plastic and plastic state. The soft soil at the estuary has typical characteristics of delta soft soil, that is, the interlaced deposition of silt and thin layers of sand. The permeability coefficient is at the level of 10-6cm/s, which is relatively low. In contrast, the permeability coefficient of silt and silty soil in most parts of the country is at the level of 10-7 to 10-8cm/s. This is the most typical feature of soft soil in this region compared to that in other areas. The porosity is relatively large, with the maximum reaching 1.91 and the minimum as low as 0.66. A high porosity ratio will inevitably affect the compressibility of the sample. When the compression coefficient is between 0.45 and 1.15, it has a relatively high compressibility. The e-p curve is shown in Figure 2.3. It can be seen from the figure that the compression curve is concave. When the loading grade is lower, the initial section of the curve is steeper and the compressibility is greater. However, as the loading grade increases, the curve becomes flatter and the compressibility gradually decreases. Compared with soil samples from deeper layers, the initial pore ratio of soil samples from shallower layers is larger and their compressibility is higher. The particle analysis curves of soil at different depths are shown in Figure 2.4. It can be seen that the particle size distribution of soft soil at the Pearl River estuary is approximately between 0.00140.1mm, with an average particle size range of approximately between 0.0140.075. It is mainly composed of clay and silt particles, and the gradation curve is relatively steep. The cumulative content corresponding to the particle size in the middle part varies greatly. The internal friction Angle is between 14.122.8, the cohesion is between 0.0536.5, and the undrained shear strength index is shown in Figure 2.5.

It can be seen from the basic physical and mechanical indicators that the soft soil at the estuary of the Pearl River can be regarded as a typical representative of the soft soil in the Pearl River Delta. Under the complex alternating action of rivers and seas, a deep interlaced sedimentary layer of silt and thin sand is formed. Due to the presence of thin layers of sand in the soil, the permeability coefficient of the soft soil in this area is generally at the level of 10-6-10-8cm/s, which is not at the same level as that of silt and silty soil in most parts of the country. This is the most typical feature of the soft soil in this area compared with that in other regions.

(1) The soft soil at the estuary of the Pearl River has significant thixotropic properties. Whether it is the cross-plate shear test or the unconfined compression test, the strength of the samples with different moisture contents after reshaping does not vary much, and with the increase of the standing time, its strength shows a certain degree of recovery. Under the same standing time, the strength gradually decreases as the moisture content increases. (2) Under different test methods, the influence of moisture content on thixotropic strength is consistent. However, the thixotropic strength recovery curves of soft soil obtained by different test methods vary, and there are significant differences in the thixotropic strength recovery of soil. Generally speaking, the thixotropic strength of soft soil obtained through the cross-plate shear test is higher than that obtained through the unconfined compression test. The thixotropic strength of the soil obtained through the cross-plate shear test is higher than that obtained through the unconfined compression test. (3) Based on the results of the cross-plate shear test, a thixotropic strength model for soft soil at the Pearl River estuary that can reflect the combined action of initial moisture content and standing time is proposed. This thixotropic strength model has good prediction effect and accuracy, which can lay an experimental foundation for the establishment of thixotropic prediction methods for soft soil at the Pearl River estuary.

This paper takes the soft soil at the estuary of the Pearl River as the research object and uses laboratory tests as the main research method. The thixotropic strength characteristics of the soft soil are studied through cross-plate shear tests and unconfined compression tests. The evolution law of the thixotropic strength of the soft soil under the influence of different standing times and moisture contents is analyzed, and a thixotropic strength model of the soft soil at the estuary is proposed based on the test results. The thixotropic stiffness characteristics of soft soil at estuaries were studied through one-dimensional compression tests, and the evolution mechanism of thixotropic stiffness of soft soil at estuaries was revealed in combination with the results of microscopic tests. Finally, a dynamic triaxial test was carried out to study the thixotropic characteristics of soft soil at the estuary under dynamic load disturbances, and the evolution laws of thixotropic strength and stiffness of soft soil under dynamic loads were analyzed. The main research contents and conclusions are as follows: Through a large number of basic physical and mechanical property tests on the soft soil at the estuary, the permeability coefficient of this soft soil is 4.75×10-7cm/s, the average cohesion is 22.7kPa, the average internal friction Angle is 6.6°, and the compression coefficient is 0.80/Mpa. The soft soil at the estuary of the Pearl River has typical properties of delta soft soil. That is, silt and thin layers of sand are deposited alternately. The permeability coefficient is generally at the level of 10-6-10-8cm/s, while in most parts of the country, the permeability coefficient of silt and silty soil is at the level of 10-7-10-8cm/s. This is the most typical feature of soft soil in this region compared to other regions. (2) The thixotropic strength characteristics of soft soil at the estuary were studied through cross-plate shear tests and unconfined compression tests, and the evolution law of thixotropic strength under the influence of different standing times and moisture contents was analyzed. The results show that for the same standing time, the strength of samples with different moisture contents decreases with the increase of moisture content. The recovery of soil thixotropic strength under different test methods shows significant differences. The thixotropic recovery strength of soil in the cross-plate shear test is stronger than that in the unconfined compression test. Based on the cross-plate shear test data, a thixotropic model of soft soil strength at the Pearl River estuary reflecting the influence of initial moisture content and standing time was established. (3) The thixotropic stiffness characteristics of soft soil at the Pearl River estuary were analyzed through one-dimensional compression tests. The effects of standing time and moisture content on the compression index and rebound index were studied. Combined with microstructure tests, the evolution mechanism of thixotropic stiffness of soft soil was revealed. The results show that the compression index decreases with the increase of standing time and shows a linear growth trend with the increase of moisture content. The rebound index does not change significantly with the standing time, but shows a linear growth trend with the increase of moisture content. The thixotropic stiffness ratio of the samples with low moisture content shows a slow growth trend, while that of the samples with high moisture content first increases rapidly and eventually stabilizes. Meanwhile, the higher the moisture content, the greater the thixotropic stiffness ratio of the corresponding soil sample, indicating that the degree of thixotropic stiffness recovery is stronger. The microstructure of undisturbed soil is mainly composed of flocculation structure. The microstructure of reshaped soil gradually changes from a dispersed structure to a flocculation structure with the increase of standing time. For the same standing time, the porosity of the soil microstructure shows a linear growth trend with the increase of moisture content, and the corresponding compression index also shows a linear growth trend with the increase of moisture content. (4) A cyclic triaxial test was conducted on the soft soil at the estuary using a GDS dynamic triaxial instrument. The influences of cyclic confining pressure and loading frequency on the deformation and pore pressure variation laws of the soft soil at the estuary were analyzed. The results show that the cumulative axial strain of the specimen decreases with the increase of cyclic confining pressure and loading frequency. Combined with the test results, the expressions of the normalized cumulative axial strain with the slope of the stress path and the loading frequency were established respectively. The maximum and minimum overhole pressures increase with the increase of the slope of the stress path, but their variation trends with the loading frequency are inconsistent. (5) The GDS dynamic triaxial instrument was used to conduct dynamic first and then static tests on the soft soil at the estuary, and the evolution laws of the shear strength and elastic modulus of the soft soil under the influence of different post-vibration reconsolidation degrees were studied. The results show that with the increase of the degree of reconsolidation, both the shear strength and elastic modulus of the undrained water have improved. Compared with the undrained strength and elastic modulus without cyclic loading, the shear strength and elastic modulus after complete reconsolidation are both improved. There exists a critical consolidation degree that makes the post-vibration shear strength and elastic modulus of soft soil equal to the shear strength and stiffness of specimens that have not undergone cyclic loading. The degree of reconsolidation has a significant influence on the shear strength and elastic modulus of soft soil at the estuary of the Pearl River after cycling. The shear strength of soil samples that have not been completely reconsolidated decreased by 27% after cycling, and the stiffness decreased by 57%.

This chapter takes the construction section of the pipe jacking of the Hexing Power Tunnel under the Nansha Port Expressway in the Nansha Free Trade Zone of Guangzhou City as the engineering background. The strength characteristics of the silt-like soil in the underpass section are obtained through indoor tests. Then, by analyzing the on-site construction process flow, the causes and laws of ground deformation during the pipe jacking construction process are studied. And the measures that can be taken to control ground deformation at different stages of jacking were summarized and explained. The main work carried out and the achievements obtained in this chapter are as follows: (1) Through the analysis of the overview and key difficulties of the pipe jacking project of the Hexing Power Tunnel, it can be known that the soft foundation treatment of the subgrade under the pipe jacking construction in the study section is carried out by the plastic drainage board surcharge preloading method. During the jacking process, special control measures need to be taken to overcome the influence of flexible obstacles and ensure the normal jacking operation of the pipe jacking machine. (2) Conventional geotechnical foundation tests were conducted on the silty soil samples within the pipe jacking construction area to obtain the basic physical property parameters of the soil samples. The compressibility index and shear strength index of the soil samples were respectively obtained through standard consolidation tests and triaxial compression tests. The compressive modulus Es1-2 was 2.96MPa, and the cohesion Cu was 10.68kPa. The internal friction Angle φ is 6.37°, providing data support for numerical simulation calculations. (3) Based on the summary and analysis of the pipe jacking process flow, it is proposed that there are two reasons for ground deformation caused by pipe jacking construction, namely soil disturbance and stratum loss. According to the differences in the stress changes of the surrounding soil at different stages of pipe jacking construction, the disturbed soil is zoned. Meanwhile, six major reasons for the formation loss caused by pipe jacking construction are summarized, and corresponding control methods are given for the formation loss caused by different reasons. (4) According to the position of the ground relative to the pipe jacking machine, the deformation is divided into five stages: initial settlement, extrusion deformation in front of the pipe jacking machine, shear deformation when the pipe jacking machine passes through, settlement after the pipe jacking machine passes through, and consolidation settlement. The causes of ground deformation at each stage were analyzed, and corresponding control measures were proposed.

This chapter is based on the construction section of the pipe jacking of the Hexing Power Tunnel in the Nansha Free Trade Zone of Guangzhou City passing under the Nansha Port Expressway. The finite element model is constructed using Midas GTS/NX software. Ten working conditions are set to optimize and analyze the two important jacking parameters of the excavation face top thrust and grouting pressure, and the optimal jacking parameters of the construction section passing under are obtained. Based on this, the influence laws of pipe jacking construction in coastal silt-like layers on the settlement and horizontal displacement of soil layers and high-speed road surfaces were studied and obtained. Finally, the implementation methods of settlement control technical measures for pipe jacking under expressways were analyzed and explained from three aspects: stability control of excavation face, grouting control, and other control measures. The work carried out and the achievements obtained in this chapter are as follows: (1) The Midas GTS/NX software was used to conduct numerical simulation calculations on the pavement settlement under different combinations of jacking thrust and grouting pressure within the study section. The influence laws of two important jacking parameters on the pavement settlement of expressways were analyzed and obtained. The results show that when the top thrust of the excavation face is taken as 120 kPa and the grouting pressure is taken as 140 kPa, it is the most beneficial for controlling the deformation of the expressway and the pavement settlement is the least. (2) Through numerical simulation calculation and analysis, the variation law of the settlement of the lower pavement affected by pipe jacking construction is obtained. Research shows that when the cutter head of the pipe jacking machine advances to 20 meters (5 times the pipe diameter) before the measurement point, the road surface begins to rise. The rise reaches its peak 5 meters (1 times the pipe diameter) before the measurement point and starts to show a settlement trend. When it advances to the measurement point, due to the disturbance of the soil caused by the pipe jacking machine and the ground loss caused by construction, a relatively large settlement rate begins to occur at each measurement point. About 15 meters (4 times the pipe diameter) after the cutter head passes through, the settlement rate slows down and enters the consolidation settlement stage. The changing trend is consistent with the theoretical analysis of the ground deformation law caused by pipe jacking construction. The post-construction settlement of the pavement shows an inverted quasi-normal distribution curve. The maximum pavement settlement occurs at the intersection point CD-1 of the expressway centerline and the tunnel axis, with a settlement value reaching -19.63mm. The influence range of pavement settlement is 24 times the pipe diameter. (3) Through numerical simulation calculation and analysis, the variation law of deep vertical displacement of soil under the influence of pipe jacking construction is obtained. After the pipe jacking machine passes through, the soil loss caused by the diameter difference between the pipe jacking machine shell and the pipe section will cause the soil above the pipe section to settle downward and the soil below the pipe section to rise upward. After the jacking was completed, the maximum settlement of the deep soil occurred at the top of the pipe section arch directly below the centerline of the expressway, with a settlement amount of -31.21mm. The maximum uplift of the deep soil occurred at the bottom of the pipe section arch 19 meters below the ramp, with a uplift value of 43.22mm. (4) Through numerical simulation calculation and analysis, the variation law of horizontal displacement under the influence of pipe jacking construction is obtained. The deep horizontal displacement of the soil is jointly affected by the tunneling pressure and soil loss. After the jacking construction is completed, the maximum value of the deep horizontal displacement of the soil occurs at the burial depth of the axis around the pipe wall. The maximum post-construction deep horizontal displacements of the soil in the five groups of sections are -26.12mm, -27.36mm, -25.76mm, and -25.32mm respectively. -24.81mm. After the pipe jacking construction was completed, the horizontal displacement of the road surface at the jacking axis was zero. The horizontal displacement of the road surface towards the pipe jacking axis gradually increased along both sides of the axis and reached a peak about 6 meters away from the axis. Subsequently, the horizontal displacement began to show a decreasing trend. The maximum horizontal displacement of the road surface occurred at the centerline of the expressway, and the displacement direction was towards the pipe jacking axis. The displacement value reached 3.23mm, and the influence range of the horizontal displacement of the road surface was 24 times the pipe diameter, which was consistent with the settlement of the road surface. (5) Based on the on-site construction situation, the implementation methods of the settlement control technical measures for pipe jacking under the expressway are analyzed from three aspects: stability control of the excavation face, grouting control and other control measures. It is proposed that when conducting pipe jacking construction in deep soft soil layers, the head of the machine can be rigidly connected to the last two pipe sections to form an integrated whole with the pipe sections, thereby avoiding the risk of head insertion when the pipe jacking machine emerges from the tunnel, improving the straightness during jacking, and at the same time increasing the weight of the rear shell to ensure precise control of the deviation correction operation.

This chapter takes the construction section of the pipe jacking of the Hexing Power Tunnel under the Nansha Port Expressway in the Nansha Free Trade Zone of Guangzhou City as the research background. Based on the analysis of the key and difficult points of the construction in the previous text, it is determined that the influence of traffic load and the removal of flexible obstacles with plastic drainage boards are the two unfavorable construction factors for the jacking construction of the underpass section. Based on the original numerical analysis model, the influence degree of pipe jacking construction on pavement deformation under the action of adverse factors was analyzed. On this basis, the treatment measures for two unfavorable construction factors in the background project, namely the traffic load and the flexible obstacle of plastic drainage board, were studied and explained. The main research conclusions are as follows: (1) A simulation simplification method is proposed for the two major construction difficulties of traffic load and plastic drainage board in the underpass expressway section. The traffic load is regarded as a triangular load considering the impact effect, and the magnitude and boundary of the load application are calculated in combination with the actual operating vehicle conditions of the expressway. The pulling effect of the drainage board on the soil is simplified as additional ground loss, and the additional thickness of the mud jacket is calculated. Considering the influence laws of pipe jacking construction on expressways under different working conditions comprehensively, the conclusion obtained is more in line with the actual engineering. (2) Based on the original numerical analysis model, the influence laws of two unfavorable construction factors on the high-speed construction of pipe jacking under were studied by adding traffic loads and additional ground losses. Research shows that the deformation laws of pavement settlement and horizontal displacement caused by pipe jacking construction under different working conditions remain unchanged, but the maximum values of pavement settlement and horizontal displacement at the centerline of the expressway both increase. When only the vehicle load is considered, the maximum road surface settlement increases by 6.36 mm, and the maximum road surface horizontal displacement increases by 0.37 mm. When the circumferential void increases by 5.00 mm, the maximum plane settlement increases by 1.38 mm and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 0.11 mm. When the circumferential clearance increases by 15.0 mm, the maximum plane settlement increases by 4.98 mm and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 0.33 mm. Under the most unfavorable working conditions, the maximum plane settlement increases by 12.05mm, and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 1.21mm. At this time, the maximum plane settlement is -31.68mm, which exceeds the allowable value of the specification. During construction, corresponding control technical measures must be taken to reduce the impact of the two adverse factors on the construction and ensure the safety of the operation. (3) When the pipe is pushed under the plastic drainage board to treat the roadbed, there is a risk of the cutter head getting stuck, the pipeline being blocked, and the ground suddenly sinking. By modifying the cutter head and adding a sharp blade tearing knife and optimizing the sludge discharge equipment, the drainage board can be effectively broken through, avoiding the adverse effects of the flexible obstacles of the plastic drainage board and effectively controlling the settlement of the road surface. (4) When pipe jacking is carried out under an operational expressway, a special traffic diversion plan can be formulated to implement temporary traffic control measures on the sections affected by the construction, thereby reducing the impact of traffic loads and controlling road surface settlement.

This chapter takes the construction section of the He Xing Power Tunnel under the Nansha Port Expressway in the Nansha Free Trade Zone of Guangzhou City as the background, and formulates a detailed monitoring plan. By analyzing the on-site measured data, it studies the laws of pavement settlement, pavement horizontal displacement, and deep soil horizontal displacement caused by pipe jacking construction, and compares the measured data with the numerical simulation results. The effectiveness of the pipe jacking construction control technical measures mentioned earlier was verified. The main research results are as follows: (1) A remote automated data collection system was introduced to replace manual data collection in the underpass expressway section, and an automated monitoring data feedback system for the pipe jacking machine's tunneling posture and the deformation of the expressway pavement was established. (2) By analyzing the monitoring data of pavement settlement, the influence law of pipe jacking construction on pavement settlement was obtained. Monitoring data show that the settlement of the measurement points at the intersection of the five groups of monitoring sections and the pipe jacking axis is the greatest. The settlement variation patterns are basically the same. Before the machine head is jacking to the end, the measurement points bulge. When passing through the section, the settlement rate of the measurement points reaches the maximum due to the influence of the pipe diameter difference. The measured post-construction settlement curves of the five monitoring sections conform to the conclusion of Peck's empirical formula. The maximum settlement all occurred above the jacking axis. The post-construction settlements at the axes of sections 1 to 5 were -21.86 mm, -15.73 mm, -10.33 mm, -9.60 mm, and -9.92 mm respectively. The affected range is twice the pipe diameter. (3) A comparative analysis was conducted on the settlement monitoring data of five monitoring sections after the pipe jacking machine passed through. It was proved that when sudden ground settlement occurs during the pipe jacking construction process, taking control measures such as increasing the grouting volume, grouting pressure, and secondary grouting can achieve a good effect of supporting the stratum and reducing subsequent settlement. (4) Through the analysis of the monitoring data of the horizontal displacement of the pavement, it was found that the distribution patterns of the post-construction horizontal displacement of the pavement at the measurement points of the five groups of monitoring sections were the same. The displacement was zero at the jacking axis, gradually increased along the axis to both sides, reached the peak at 6 meters away from the axis, and then began to show a decreasing trend. The maximum horizontal displacement values of the plane from Section 1 to Section 5 after work are 3.13mm, 2.61mm, 2.13mm, 1.62mm and 1.83mm respectively. (5) Through the analysis of the monitoring data of the deep horizontal displacement of the soil, it was found that as the pipe jacking machine advanced, the horizontal displacement at the measurement point gradually increased. The rate of change of the horizontal displacement reached the highest when jacking to the measurement point. 5 meters after passing the measurement point, the displacement value began to stabilize. After the pipe jacking construction was completed, the maximum horizontal displacement at the pipe section axis was 2.65mm. The direction is away from the pipe wall. (6) The on-site monitoring data was compared and analyzed with the numerical simulation results. The reasons for the differences between the two were analyzed in combination with the actual construction situation on site, which proved the effectiveness of the construction control measures for the underpass expressway section in this paper and can provide a reference for pipe jacking projects in similar situations.

This paper is based on the construction section where the pipe jacking of the Hexing Power Tunnel in the Nansha Free Trade Zone of Guangzhou City passes under the Nansha Port Expressway. It adopts research methods such as theoretical analysis, indoor experiments, numerical simulation and on-site monitoring. The research was conducted respectively on the deformation law of the road surface under the expressway during pipe jacking construction, the influence of traffic load and plastic drainage board clearance on pipe jacking construction, the automatic monitoring information feedback system of pipe jacking tunneling posture and road surface settlement, etc. The settlement control technical measures for pipe jacking under the operational expressway were systematically proposed. Finally, the effectiveness of the control measures was verified in combination with the on-site measured data. The main research achievements are as follows: By analyzing the background engineering geological characteristics and engineering features, the key and difficult points of the construction of pipe jacking under the expressway within the research section were proposed. Standard consolidation tests and triaxial compression tests were conducted on the silty soil samples in the construction area, and the compressive modulus Es1-2 of the soil was obtained as 2.96 MPa, and the cohesion Cu was 10.68 kPa. The internal friction Angle φ is 6.37 °. Based on the analysis of the on-site pipe jacking construction process flow, the mechanism of ground deformation caused by construction was studied. The results show that soil disturbance and stratum loss are the two major causes of ground deformation caused by pipe jacking construction. According to the change of the spatial position of the pipe jacking machine's advancement, the ground settlement caused by pipe jacking construction was divided into five stages, the causes of settlement in each stage were obtained, and corresponding control measures were proposed. (2) By using the numerical simulation method, the two important jacking parameters, namely the top thrust of the excavation face and the grouting pressure, during the pipe jacking construction process were optimized and analyzed. The results show that when the top thrust of the excavation face is set at 120 kPa and the grouting pressure is set at 140 kPa, it is the most beneficial for controlling the deformation of the expressway and the pavement settlement is the least. Based on this, the variation law of pavement settlement during pipe jacking under the optimal jacking parameters was studied. The results show that when the pipe jacking machine jacked to five times the pipe diameter before the measurement point, the pavement began to rise. When it jacked to one times the pipe diameter before the measurement point, the rise reached its peak and a settlement trend emerged. When it jacked to the measurement point, a relatively large rate of settlement began to occur. After the cutterhead passes through, the settlement rate slows down approximately 4 times the pipe diameter and enters the consolidation settlement stage. The maximum pavement settlement occurs at the intersection point CD-1 of the expressway centerline and the tunnel axis, with a settlement value reaching -19.63mm. The influence range of pavement settlement is 2 to 4 times the pipe diameter. (3) The variation law of the horizontal displacement during the construction of pipe jacking under the expressway was analyzed by numerical simulation. Among them, the deep horizontal displacement of the soil was jointly affected by the tunneling pressure and soil loss. After the jacking construction was completed, the maximum value of the deep horizontal displacement of the soil occurred at the burial depth of the pipe wall's peripheral axis. The maximum horizontal displacements of the deep soil layer after work in the five groups of sections were -26.12 mm, -27.36 mm, -25.76 mm, -25.32 mm, and -24.81 mm respectively. After the pipe jacking construction was completed, the horizontal displacement of the road surface at the jacking axis was zero. The horizontal displacement of the road surface towards the pipe jacking axis gradually increased along both sides of the axis and reached a peak about 6 meters away from the axis. Subsequently, the horizontal displacement began to show a decreasing trend. The maximum horizontal displacement of the road surface occurred at the centerline of the expressway, and the displacement direction was towards the pipe jacking axis. The displacement value reaches 3.23mm. (4) The deformation law of the pavement at the centerline of the expressway under the influence of two unfavorable construction factors, namely traffic load and plastic drainage board, was calculated by numerical simulation method. The results show that the deformation laws of pavement settlement and horizontal displacement caused by pipe jacking construction under different working conditions have not changed, but both the maximum settlement and horizontal displacement of the pavement have significantly increased: when only the traffic load is considered, the maximum pavement settlement increases by 6.36 mm, and the maximum pavement horizontal displacement increases by 0.37 mm. When the circumferential void increases by 5.00 mm, the maximum plane settlement increases by 1.38 mm and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 0.11 mm. When the circumferential void increases by 15.00 mm, the maximum plane settlement increases by 4.98 mm and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 0.33 mm. Under the most unfavorable working conditions, the maximum plane settlement increases by 12.05 mm, and the maximum plane horizontal displacement increases by 1.21 mm. Targeted control measures need to be taken during construction to reduce the impact of the two adverse factors on the construction and ensure construction safety. (5) Settlement control measures for the construction of pipe jacking under expressways were proposed from three aspects: stability control of the excavation face, grouting control and other control measures. On this basis, measures such as modifying the cutter head to add a sharp blade tearing knife, optimizing the sludge discharge equipment, and setting up a road settlement early warning and emergency response mechanism are proposed, which can effectively eliminate the adverse effects of the flexible obstacles of the plastic drainage board. (6) According to the characteristics of the project, an on-site monitoring plan was formulated. An automatic monitoring information feedback system for the tunneling posture of the pipe jacking machine and the deformation of the expressway pavement was established and applied to the on-site project. Through the analysis of the on-site monitoring data, the effectiveness and rationality of the construction control measures for the underpass expressway section in this paper were verified. The conclusions obtained can provide a reference for pipe jacking projects in similar situations.

Based on the soft soil treatment project of Dalian Changxing Island Industrial Park, indoor model tests were carried out for the dredging and fill soil in this area. The test instrument used was the cone drop meter in the Institute of Geotechnical Engineering, Beiyangyuan Campus of Tianjin University. The sample samples were taken from the undisturbed silt soil at a depth of 2.7m-2.9m in the area and prepared into six groups of undisturbed soil samples with a diameter of 50mm and a height of 25mm. Disturbed soil was prepared from undisturbed silty soil. Every two groups of soil samples were disturbed for 10 minutes, 15 minutes, and 25 minutes respectively to simulate different degrees of disturbance. This experimental process focused on analyzing the strength recovery laws of soil samples under different degrees of disturbance and different standing times (0, 7, 15, 25, and 30 days), and obtained the strength recovery factors. The main conclusions of this chapter are as follows: (1) Due to the disturbance of the soil sample, the original structure was damaged. After being placed for a period of time and rearranged, the damaged structure gradually transformed into a stable state, and the structural strength slowly recovered. Therefore, the undrained shear strength of dredged fill soil gradually increases with the extension of the standing time, and the strength shows a linear growth trend with time. As the degree of disturbance increases, the arrangement of particles within the soil sample becomes more disordered and the deformation greater, resulting in a smaller contact area between soil particles, a weaker cementation between particles, and a smaller undischarged shear strength of the soil sample. At the same time, the shear strength and the degree of disturbance show a linear decreasing trend. (2) As the soil sample is disturbed, the cemence between soil particles will be disrupted. After being placed for a period of time and rearranged, the thixotropic strength gradually increases over time and eventually stabilizes. Therefore, the longer the standing time, the greater the thixotropic recovery strength ratio of the dredged fill soil, and the thixotropic recovery strength ratio has a linear positive correlation with time. As the degree of disturbance increases, the microstructure within the soil becomes more disordered, the contact area between particles decreases, and the relative degree of strength recovery also becomes smaller. Therefore, the greater the degree of disturbance, the smaller the thixotropic recovery intensity ratio. The thixotropic recovery intensity ratio has a linear negative correlation with the degree of disturbance. (3) The degree of strength recovery of dredged fill soil increases with the extension of the standing time, and the degree of strength recovery shows a linear growth trend with time. Based on different degrees of disturbance, the strength recovery factor of dredged fill soil is obtained for the relationship expression between the degree of strength recovery and the standing time.

Based on the soft soil foundation treatment project of Dalian Changxing Island Industrial Park, an indoor model test was carried out on the dredging and fill soil in this area. The test instrument used the cone drop meter in the Institute of Geotechnical Engineering, Beiyangyuan Campus of Tianjin University. The sample samples were taken from the original silt soil with depths of 0.7-0.9m, 1.7-1.9m and 3.7-3.9m respectively and the reshaped soil samples with moisture contents of 30%, 35% and 40% respectively in the area. They were respectively prepared into six groups of soil samples with diameters of 50mm and heights of 25mm. Disturbed soil was prepared from undisturbed soil samples and reshaped soil samples respectively. Every two groups of soil samples were disturbed for a duration of 15 minutes to simulate the degree of disturbance. This test process focused on analyzing the influences of different depths and different moisture contents on the undrained shear strength, thixotropic recovery strength ratio and strength recovery degree of soil samples respectively, providing theoretical guidance for the actual engineering in this area. The main conclusions of this chapter are as follows: (1) The undrained shear strength of the dredged and filled soil samples gradually increases with the increase of depth; However, they all gradually decrease as the moisture content increases. (2) When the standing time is 0 days, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio of dredged fill soil does not change with the increase of depth and moisture content. When the standing time was 7 days, 15 days, 25 days and 30 days, the deeper the depth of the soil samples taken, the greater the thixotropic recovery strength ratio, but the increase was extremely small. However, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio shows a trend of first decreasing and then increasing with the increase of moisture content. (3) When the standing time is 0 days, the degree of strength recovery of dredged fill soil does not change with the increase of depth and moisture content. When the standing time was 7 days, 15 days, 25 days and 30 days, the degree of strength recovery increased with the increase of the depth of the soil samples taken, but the increase was very small. The greater the moisture content, the greater the degree of strength recovery.

Based on the soft soil foundation treatment project in Dalian Changxing Island Industrial Park, a finite element analysis of the three-dimensional model was first carried out for the failure mode and bearing characteristics of the hard shell soft soil foundation. Constitutive models suitable for soft soil layers and hard shell materials were selected, and the model theory was briefly introduced. Secondly, a parameter analysis was conducted on the relevant factors influencing the bearing capacity of the foundation by the hard shell layer. The effects of the strength, thickness, size, burial depth, nonlinear distribution of the hard shell layer and the load of the overlaid foundation on the failure mode, plastic strain and ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation were analyzed. The main conclusions of this chapter are as follows: (1) The hard crust layer has a stress diffusion effect, which enhances its load-bearing capacity and the deformation coordination ability of the soil layer. Therefore, under the same load, as the depth of the foundation increases, the displacement deformation will show a gradually decreasing trend. (2) The failure mode of the foundation remains almost unchanged as the strength of the hard shell layer itself increases and the load of the overlying foundation rises, all being shear failure. With the increase of the burial depth of the hard shell layer and the nonlinear distribution, local shear failure is presented. (3) When the hard shell layer is relatively thick, local shear failure mainly occurs. When the hard shell layer is relatively thin, if the size of the hard shell layer is large enough, it will mainly suffer from shear failure; otherwise, the soil of the foundation will tend to undergo local shear failure. (4) The strength, thickness, size, burial depth, nonlinear distribution and overburden load of the hard shell layer have a significant impact on the variation of the ultimate bearing capacity of the soft soil hard shell layer foundation. Generally speaking, under the center of the base, the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation increases with the increase of depth. The stronger the strength, the thicker the thickness, the larger the size, the shallower the burial depth and the smaller the overburden load, the greater the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation. The more stable and safe the foundation is.

Firstly, several main theoretical formulas for calculating the ultimate bearing capacity of a foundation with a hard top and a soft bottom were introduced, and their applicable scopes were expounded. Subsequently, by analyzing the special engineering properties of the hard shell layer, the theory of shear failure was theoretically revised. Finally, based on the soft soil foundation treatment project in the Changxing Island area of Dalian, the test results were compared and analyzed with the theoretical values and corrected values, verifying the feasibility of the modified calculation formula for the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation. The main conclusions of this chapter are as follows: (1) The strength-weighted average method is applicable to calculating the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation when the force-bearing layer is composed of multiple soil layers and the strength indicators of each soil layer do not differ much. However, it is no longer applicable to hard shell soft soil foundations where the physical and mechanical indicators of each soil layer vary greatly. (2) The method for calculating the ultimate bearing capacity of a foundation based on the theory of shear failure takes into account the situation where the soil contains weak layers. However, its premise is to assume that the hard crust layer undergoes shear failure and that the shear failure develops in a vertical downward direction. However, due to the special engineering properties of the hard shell layer, the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation in actual engineering is generally higher than the calculated value in the theory of shear failure. Therefore, it is inevitable to revise the theoretical formula of shear failure. (3) By comprehensively considering the sealing characteristics of the hard shell layer, the effect of the reverse pressure retaining wall and the stress diffusion effect, the theory of shear failure was revised, and a new prediction model for the ultimate bearing capacity of the double-layer foundation with hard top and soft bottom was obtained. (4) The calculation results of the new model for predicting the ultimate bearing capacity of double-layer foundations with hard top and soft bottom are closer to the on-site test values compared with the theoretical values of shear failure.

(1) The undrained shear strength of dredged fill soil gradually increases with the increase of standing time, showing a linear growth trend. Meanwhile, with the increase of time, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio of dredged and fill soil gradually increases, and the two show a linear positive correlation. In addition, the degree of strength recovery of dredged fill soil will increase with the extension of the standing time, and it also shows a linear growth trend. When the degree of disturbance increases, the undrained shear strength of dredged fill soil will decrease, showing a linear decreasing trend. Meanwhile, the greater the degree of disturbance, the relatively smaller the ratio of thixotropic recovery intensity will be. The ratio of thixotropic recovery intensity shows a linear negative correlation with the degree of disturbance. (2) Based on different degrees of disturbance, the strength recovery factor of the soil is obtained by establishing the relationship expression between the strength recovery degree and the static time. (3) The undrained shear strength of dredged fill soil gradually increases with the increase of depth. When the standing time is 0 days, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio of dredged fill remains unchanged when the depth changes. However, when the standing time is 7 days, 15 days, 25 days, and 30 days, the greater the depth of the sampled soil, the slightly increased thixotropic recovery intensity ratio will be, but the extent of the increase is very small. Similarly, when the standing time is 0 days, the degree of strength recovery of dredged fill soil remains unchanged when the depth changes. However, when the standing time was 7 days, 15 days, 25 days, and 30 days, the degree of strength recovery increased with the increase of soil sample depth, but the increase was very small. (4) The undrained shear strength of dredged fill soil gradually decreases with the increase of moisture content. When the standing time is 0 days, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio of the dredged fill soil remains unchanged when the moisture content changes. However, when the standing time was 7 days, 15 days, 25 days, and 30 days, the thixotropic recovery strength ratio showed a trend of first decreasing and then increasing with the increase of moisture content. Similarly, when the standing time is 0 days, the degree of strength recovery of the dredged fill soil remains unchanged when the moisture content changes. However, when the standing time is 7 days, 15 days, 25 days and 30 days, the higher the moisture content, the greater the degree of strength recovery. Under the same load, as the depth of the foundation increases, the displacement deformation gradually decreases. Whether the strength of the hard shell layer increases or the load of the overlying foundation increases, the failure mode is always shear failure. However, with the increase of the burial depth of the hard shell and the existence of nonlinear distribution, local shear failure will also emerge. If the hard shell layer is relatively thick, local shear failure mainly occurs. When the hard crust is relatively thin, if the size of the hard crust is large enough, mainly shear failure will occur; otherwise, the soil of the foundation will tend to undergo local shear failure. The strength, thickness, size, burial depth, nonlinear distribution and overburden load of the hard shell layer have a significant impact on the variation of the ultimate bearing capacity of the soft soil hard shell layer foundation. Under the center of the base, as the depth increases, the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation also increases. Meanwhile, the stronger the strength, thicker the thickness, larger the size, shallower the burial depth and smaller the overburden of the hard shell layer, the greater the ultimate bearing capacity of the foundation, thus making the foundation more stable and safe. (6) Based on the engineering characteristics of hard shells, the theoretical formula for shear failure was revised, and a new prediction model for the ultimate bearing capacity of double-layer foundations with hard top and soft bottom was obtained. The calculation results of the new model for predicting the ultimate bearing capacity of double-layer foundations with hard top and soft bottom are closer to the on-site test values compared with the theoretical values of shear failure.

After remodeling, the thixotropic strength of the silt in Taihu Lake and Baima Lake increased the fastest in the initial stage. With the increase of time, the growth rate of thixotropic strength gradually slowed down. The turning points for the thixotropic strength growth of both types of silt were approximately 112 days. By 448 days, the thixotropic strength had basically stabilized over time. The lower the moisture content, the greater the thixotropic strength at the same time. Meanwhile, the lower the moisture content, the more obvious the influence of moisture content changes on thixotropic strength. The changing trend of the thixotropic strength ratio is similar to that of the thixotropic strength. When the moisture content is at the liquid limit, the thixotropic strength and thixotropic strength ratio of the silt in Baima Lake are slightly higher than those of the silt in Taihu Lake. Overall, the lower the moisture content, the greater the thixotropy of the silt. The thixotropic intensity ratio of the silt in Taihu Lake is close to that of the silt in Baima Lake. Its value is similar to that of the silt in London and Detroit, but significantly smaller than that of the silt in Beauharnois and Shellhave, and at the same time significantly larger than that of the silt in Horten. At approximately 448 days, except for the thixotropic intensity ratio of Beauharnois silt which still maintained a continuous and stable growth, the thixotropic intensity ratios of the other silt remained relatively stable. According to the definition of soil sensitivity, both Taihu Lake silt and Baima Lake silt belong to low-sensitivity clay.

This paper aims to study the variation law and influencing factors of thixotropy of underconsolidated soil. Relying on the planned channel silt treatment project of Dalian Changxing Island Industrial Park, an indoor model test was carried out on the silty soil in this area. The experiment used indoor compaction hammers to disturb the soil samples to simulate different degrees of disturbance. Under the conditions of disturbance times of 10 min, 15 min and 25 min respectively, the drop cone test was conducted on silt soil. The recovery laws of soil sample strength under different standing times (0 d, 7 d, 15 d, 25 d, 30 d) were analyzed emphatically, and the strength recovery factors were obtained thereby. Under different degrees of disturbance, the undrained shear strength, thixotropic recovery strength ratio and strength recovery degree of the silty soil on Changxing Island gradually increase with the increase of standing time. Under the same standing time, these indicators gradually decrease with the increase of disturbance degree. 2) Under different degrees of disturbance, the strength recovery degree, thixotropic recovery strength ratio and undrained shear strength of soil samples all show a linear relationship with the standing time. Based on the expression of the relationship between the degree of strength recovery and the standing time under different degrees of disturbance, the strength recovery factor of the silt soil on Changxing Island was obtained.

Through the unconfined compressive strength test, it can be known that the sensitivity of the fourth layer of silty clay in Shanghai is approximately 1.8 times that of the on-site cross plate test result, which is very close to the 2.0 value given by Fan Xiangyang et al. [5]. This difference has a significant impact on the classification of soil layer structural grades. According to the results of the unconfined compressive strength test, this soil layer should be classified as highly sensitive soil. However, it is inappropriate to determine it as moderately sensitive soil merely based on the in-situ cross plate test. The results of the indoor micro-cross plate test show that the reshaping mode of the soil sample is the main reason for the significant difference in sensitivity between the two tests. The unconfined compressive strength test adopts manual reshaping, and the reshaping strength is smaller than that of the cross plate test, so the measured sensitivity is higher. The thixotropic recovery test results show that during the 6-month static period, the sensitivity St increased to approximately one-third of the sensitivity measured in the unconfined compressive strength test. Therefore, unless the structure is stable, thixotropy is also one of the main reasons why the fourth layer of silty clay in Shanghai shows high sensitivity. The soft clay in Shanghai belongs to Marine sedimentary strata. Gong Shiliang [10] and Gong Shiliang and MAO Hongmei [14] analyzed the hydrochemical properties of soft clay in Shanghai and believed that due to the excessively high concentration of electrolyte (NaCl), this soil underwent flocculation and precipitation in a Marine sedimentary environment, forming a flocculation structure. Later underground freshwater action leads to a decrease in electrolyte concentration. This leaching effect of reduced salt content (leaching) may also be one of the causes of the high sensitivity of Shanghai silty clay. The Marine sedimentary soft clays in various coastal areas of our country have certain similarities in geological genesis and mechanical properties. The research results of this study can provide a reference for the structural discrimination and genesis research of soft clays in coastal areas.

The sensitive variation characteristics of saturated silt and clay with particle sizes less than 0.005mm to earthquakes and external force disturbances are called thixotropy. Earthquake-triggered soft sediment flow deformation structures include two major categories: liquefaction flow deformation and thixotropic flow deformation. The former mostly refers to the liquefaction flow deformation of sand layers and carbonate sediments, while the latter refers to the thixotropic flow deformation of saturated argillaceous sediments. In stratigraphic sections, thixotropic flow deformation structures of viscous sediments such as saturated silt, silty soil, clay, silica mud (colloidal), and carbonate plaster are widely distributed. They often form composite deformation structures with the liquefaction deformation of sand layers and the like. However, both Chinese and foreign geologists pay less attention to thixotropic flow deformation structures and often generally interpret them as liquefaction flow structures. In recent years, the thixotropic flow deformation phenomenon of saturated silt triggered by earthquakes has gradually attracted the attention of geologists. The author described and explained the causes of the flow deformation records of saturated silt triggered by earthquakes in multiple domestic stratigraphic sections. According to the directionality of thixotropic flow deformation, four types of patterns were summarized, namely (1) upward flow, (2) downward flow, (3) simultaneous upward and downward flow, and (4) near-horizontal flow, hoping to attract the attention of those engaged in the research of soft sediment deformation and paleoseismology The concern of qualitologists.

The strength of the pile body of each cement mixing pile with different proportions increases with the increase of depth, and the strength of the pile body at the same depth also increases with the increase of cement proportion. However, the strength of the pile body of each cement mixing pile with different proportions at each depth is less than 300 kPa, which does not meet the design requirements. ⑵ The test results of the single-pile bearing capacity of each cement dosage mixing pile were all poor, with an overall pass rate of only 20%. Among the four cement dosages, the highest pass rate was 75 kg/m, reaching 43%. However, the tests of the mixing piles with cement dosages of 70 kg/m and 65 kg/m were both unqualified. ⑶ The test results of the bearing capacity of the composite foundation of mixing piles with different cement dosages were good, with an overall pass rate of 80%. Among the four cement dosages, the highest pass rate was 70 kg/m, and all were qualified. The lowest pass rate is 65 kg/m, which is only 60%. ⑷ Through the improvement of the drill bit, it was found that the spiral drill bit was prone to being wrapped by silt in this project, forming a clustering phenomenon, which led to a poor pile formation effect. The pile-forming effect of the flat-head I-shaped drill bit with welded reinforcing bars has been significantly improved. From the initial observation of the core samples, it can be seen that the core samples are continuous, complete, hard, evenly mixed and have good uniformity. The bearing capacity tests all meet the design requirements, indicating that the pile-forming effect of the improved drill bit is good. ⑸ Supplementary tests on the bearing capacity of individual piles in the test section revealed that, except for the bearing capacity of the 70 kg/m ratio cement mixing piles which did not meet the design requirements, all others had reached the design requirements, with an overall pass rate of 75%. (6) To ensure the pass rate of the vertical compressive static load test of a single pile, it is recommended that the test be conducted at a later time to guarantee the full hydration of the cement. The bearing capacity value of the composite foundation and the settlement value at the top of the pile under the maximum test load can directly reflect the effect of foundation reinforcement. When the bearing capacity of the composite foundation is large and the settlement at the top of the pile is small, it indicates that the foundation reinforcement effect is good and uneven settlement is less likely to occur in the later stage [10].

In municipal pipe jacking projects, turbid water from rivers is often used to prepare bentonite slurry to save water. However, its high sand content and silt may deteriorate the performance of the slurry. It is urgent to systematically evaluate the impact of turbid water and silt on the drag reduction and wall protection characteristics of the slurry. Taking the muddy water and silt of the Yongjiang River as the research objects, 18 slurry ratios were designed through indoor orthogonal experiments. The effects of bentonite, the mass fraction of Yongjiang River silt and xanthan gum on the viscosity of the slurry funnel, filtration loss and mud skin thickness under the conditions of clear water and muddy water of the Yongjiang River were systematically tested. The results show that the mass fraction of bentonite has a significant influence on the performance indicators of thixotropic mud. The performance indicators of mud prepared with clear water increase with the increase of the mass fraction of bentonite. Muddy water reduces the viscosity of the funnel due to the weakening of the cementation effect caused by suspended silt, but it can still be used as a material for preparing slurry. Adding xanthan gum with a mass fraction of 0.05% can significantly enhance the performance of mud containing silt. When the mass fraction of silt is lower than 6%, it can meet all the index requirements of thixotropic mud.
